Identity.History: The double-hulled canoe was a revolutionary design in Polynesian and Māori maritime technology. Two hulls connected by a platform provided stability for ocean voyages and combat operations. The design allowed for greater cargo capacity and a more stable platform for warriors armed with spears and projectiles. These vessels were instrumental in Māori exploration, trade, and inter-tribal warfare, capable of navigating both coastal waters and open ocean. The double hull design made them faster and more maneuverable than single-hulled alternatives.
